title: Workspace agent-facing simplification
larger_intended_outcome: Advance #1389 by making Agentic Workspace feel like one compact skill-guided repo-native continuity loop, with the CLI as a deterministic aid layer for facts, checks, routing, proof selection, diagnostics, and safe writes rather than as the workflow itself.

parent_acceptance.original_intent: Simplify agent-facing surfaces and workflows from the highest abstraction downward while preserving AW as a repo-native continuity and execution layer for intent, context, proof, and handoff.
parent_acceptance.acceptance_target: The installed product has a reviewed target operating model, prioritized child lanes, concrete bounded implementation slices, and later visible simplifications that reduce overlap, ambiguity, and ordinary startup burden without blurring Planning, Memory, Verification, package, repo, module, local-only, generated, and catalogue/plugin ownership boundaries.
parent_acceptance.parent_proof_required: Parent closure requires installed host-repo evidence: before/after inventory of primary agent-facing surfaces, reviewed ordinary-path simplification, proof that CLI-mirroring and fallback surfaces are not ordinary duplicate payload, ownership-boundary confirmation, and at least one realistic startup or continuation walkthrough.
parent_acceptance.residual_intent_rule: Each promoted lane must state which part of #1389 remains unsatisfied and must not claim parent closure unless visible installed-product simplification has landed and been reviewed.
parent_acceptance.clarification_needed_when: Keep #1389 open or ask for owner confirmation if a slice can only produce a model, inventory, or local cleanup without evidence that ordinary agent-facing complexity is reduced.

non_goals: Do not implement broad command, docs, skill, package, or module rewrites before the target operating and participation models are explicit.
Do not add new visible concepts unless they absorb, replace, or background older visible surfaces.
Do not turn AW into runtime orchestration, project management, a plugin platform detached from repo-native workflow, or a documentation framework.
Do not treat the CLI command list itself as the workflow.
Do not preserve historical surfaces only because they already exist.

candidate_lanes.title: Define open module participation and workflow obligations
candidate_lanes.outcome: A reviewed model explains how the recommended default loop composes with repo workflow obligations, arbitrary module contributions, task posture, and dynamic instruction routing without hard-coding today's first-party modules, fixed slots, or static AGENTS.md detail.
candidate_lanes.owner_surface: src/agentic_workspace/contracts/module_registry.json, src/agentic_workspace/contracts/skill_specs.json, workflow obligation config/report surfaces, task posture/startup projection surfaces, docs/package/modules.md, docs/package/installed-surfaces.md, and first-party module README evidence
candidate_lanes.proof: Reviewed design or issue comment answers the #1392 acceptance questions, maps Planning/Memory/Verification as first-party examples of a generic participation contract, and covers task posture packets, dynamic instruction projection, module-declared posture contributions, authority boundaries, output/review posture, and closeout/report visibility.
candidate_lanes.slice_contribution_to_parent: Prevents #1389 simplification from overfitting to the current Planning/Memory/Verification surfaces or static AGENTS.md instructions before classifying ordinary workflow exposure.
candidate_lanes.residual_parent_intent: The #1392 model/contract/docs/test slice may close in PR #1393, but checked-in #1392 follow-up slices still own runtime task-posture emission, shared task_posture_packet schema synchronization, closeout/report posture adherence visibility, and dynamic AGENTS.md projection semantics. The ordinary loop classification, per-step simplification, implementation slices, and installed-product proof also remain after this lane.

candidate_lanes.title: Define the ordinary agent continuity loop and classify visible surfaces
candidate_lanes.outcome: A reviewed target ordinary workflow and surface classification exists for startup, active work, durable knowledge, proof, and closeout under the skills-first, CLI-as-aid model.
candidate_lanes.proof: Classification evidence considers all surfaces named in #1390 and produces concrete follow-on recommendations without implementing command or deletion changes.
candidate_lanes.slice_contribution_to_parent: Creates the top-level simplification target and prevents scattered local cleanup.
candidate_lanes.residual_parent_intent: Per-step substep simplification, concrete implementation issues, visible installed-product reduction, and walkthrough proof remain.

candidate_lanes.title: Investigate simplification of operating-loop substeps
candidate_lanes.outcome: Each operating-loop step has a compact reviewed inventory of skill/protocol responsibility, required agent decision, CLI aids, reasoning guidance, CLI-mirroring artifacts, state owners, fallback path, merge/remove candidates, and follow-on issue.
candidate_lanes.proof: Reviewed per-step inventories cover startup, active work, proof/verification, closeout, and durable knowledge in the order requested by #1391 or explain any deviation.
candidate_lanes.slice_contribution_to_parent: Turns the high-level model into concrete simplification choices without jumping straight to opportunistic command or docs changes.
candidate_lanes.residual_parent_intent: Concrete implementation slices and final installed-product simplification proof remain.

candidate_lanes.title: Implement bounded surface reductions from reviewed recommendations
candidate_lanes.outcome: After lane-1391-operating-loop-substeps is closed, concrete mergers, removals, backgrounding, generated/catalogue boundaries, routing changes, or command-output compression land through #1404, #1405, #1406, #1407, #1408, or a reviewed cross-cutting slice with exact proof and parent-residual reporting.
candidate_lanes.owner_surface: #1404 startup protocol-first exposure, #1405 active-work boundaries, #1406 proof adequacy, #1407 closeout protocol, #1408 durable-knowledge flow; likely touched surfaces include docs, skills, startup adapter, CLI compact outputs, module reports, generated/catalogue boundaries, and fallback surfaces.
candidate_lanes.proof: Each implementation slice runs changed-path proof plus installed-surface or host-repo walkthrough evidence appropriate to the touched surface.
candidate_lanes.slice_contribution_to_parent: Moves #1389 from reviewed model to visible installed-product simplification.
candidate_lanes.residual_parent_intent: Final before/after inventory and realistic startup or continuation walkthrough remain until enough slices have landed.

candidate_lanes.title: Review installed ordinary workflow and parent closeout evidence
candidate_lanes.outcome: The final #1389 review demonstrates that the primary agent path is smaller or more clearly routed, ownership boundaries remain sharp, and ordinary agents can start, continue, prove, and close out without assembling the system from broad manuals or command lists.
candidate_lanes.proof: Before/after inventory, review findings, and walkthrough evidence satisfy #1389 final completion requirements.
candidate_lanes.slice_contribution_to_parent: Provides the parent-closure evidence and prevents local simplifications from being mistaken for full lane satisfaction.
candidate_lanes.residual_parent_intent: None only if all parent acceptance targets are satisfied or explicitly rejected/deferred with owner.
human_confirmation_needed: Confirm whether the installed workflow is visibly simpler enough to close #1389.

dependency_assumptions: #1392 precedes #1390 and #1391 because the open participation and task-posture model prevents later simplification from freezing today's first-party modules, static AGENTS.md instructions, or fixed slots.
#1390 precedes #1391 because the per-step investigation should simplify within the reviewed ordinary-loop model instead of redesigning it.
Implementation slices are deferred until #1390 and #1391 produce reviewed recommendations with exact surface owners and proof expectations.
Parent #1389 closure is blocked until visible installed-product simplification and host-repo walkthrough evidence exist, not merely a planning model.
parallelization_assumptions: #1392 should be handled first and kept model-shaped; it may produce contract/schema/doc follow-up criteria without immediately implementing every runtime projection or changing module internals.
Once #1390 establishes the top-level classification, #1391 per-step investigations can potentially be split by loop step if they keep the common structure and dependency on #1390 evidence.
Implementation slices may run in parallel only after their affected surfaces and proof lanes are independent and parent residual intent is explicit.
proof_expectations: Planning-only lanes must cite the issue acceptance criteria and produce reviewed artifacts or issue comments rather than product rewrites.
Implementation lanes must use changed-path proof selection and include installed host-repo perspective where the change affects agent-facing surfaces.
Every lane closeout must state whether it is slice-only, advances parent acceptance, or contributes to parent closure.
Final #1389 proof must include before/after surface inventory, ordinary path walkthrough, fallback/CLI-mirroring duplication review, and ownership-boundary confirmation.
promotion_rule: Promote only the next prerequisite lane into a first-class lane. After lane-1391-operating-loop-substeps is closed, the next implementation priority is #1404 unless #1389 is explicitly re-ordered with evidence; #1405, #1406, #1407, and #1408 remain queued implementation inputs from the reviewed #1391 inventory.
notes: Created from refreshed GitHub issue evidence on 2026-06-09. Updated by #1391 work on 2026-06-10 to route reviewed implementation follow-ups through #1404, #1405, #1406, #1407, and #1408 while keeping #1389 open until installed-product simplification proof exists.
